IvCalc.title: IV Calc for Pokémon Sleep
IvCalc.description: Using this tool is easy! Simply enter your pokemon, level and skill. You will know its individual values such as RP and strength.
IvCalc.notice detail1: Let's check the future RP and Strength of the Pokémon.
IvCalc.notice detail2: Please check our tutorial videos on our <YouTube>YouTube channel</YouTube>.

go to strength tab to show the current c: Please go to the [$t(strength2)] tab to display the strength, ingredients and skill count corresponding to the current conditions.
the amount under the following condition: We are showing the amount under the following conditions.
use strength tab if you want to change t: If you want to change these conditions, please use the [$t(strength2)] tab.
berry strength: Berry strength (determined by the type of berry and the Pokémon level)
berry count: The amount of Berries per help
bonus factor: Bonus factor (Energy nature factor <nature/> × Subskill factor <subskill/>)
ingredient strength: Ingredient strength (average strength per ingredient help)
ingredient factor: Ingredient factor (determined by the Pokémon level)
great success rate: Probability of a single great success before starting research
other team member info1: Used to calculate $t(strength2) for [$t(subskill.Helping Bonus)] and $t(main skill) (such as $t(skills.Extra Helpful S.name)).
other team member info2: Calculated assuming the other 4 members of the team are this Pokémon. Note that the number of helping bonuses uses the value set for [$t(subskill.Helping Bonus)].
other member calculation: The strength is calculated assuming the other 4 members of the team are [$t(other team member)] in the [$t(parameter)] tab.
max skill level affected: Calculated at skill level <level/> because [$t(calc with max skill level)] is enabled.
skill level bonus affected: Calculated at skill level <level/> due to <bonus/>.
skill strength: Skill strength (determined by the type of main skill and skill level)
rp formula: This site is using RP formula elucidated by Goobliss, Harold, Jeancory in the Mathcode server on Discord. For more details about the formula, please read <link/> on Pokémon Sleep Info Wiki.
estimated beyond level: The value beyond level {{level}} is estimated.
rate is not fixed: The ingredient% and skill% for this Pokémon are not yet confirmed, so provisional values are displayed.
rate is not fixed title: {{name}}'s stats are not confirmed. You can simulate by changing the provisional values.
additional candy description: Chance to obtain additional candy (from $t(skill level) 6)
confirm add to box: Are you sure you want to add this Pokémon to the box?
pokemon in the box is edited: The Pokémon in the box have been edited. Please save to apply the changes to the box.
box is full: The box is full. Please delete the unnecessary Pokémon.
box is empty: No Pokémon have been registered yet.
no pokemon found: No Pokémon matching the specified criteria were found.
export message1: The Pokémon information in the box is saved in the browser and will not be transferred to another environment.
export message2: To transfer the Pokémon in the box to another environment, please import the following data into the target environment.
export notice: It is recommended to periodically export the Pokémon data in the box.
import clipboard: Paste the exported data and press the [Import] button. (Supported formats: Simple / CSV / TSV)
import message file: You can import files exported by the export feature. (Supported formats: Simple / CSV / TSV)
import message notice: For CSV/TSV format, import is possible even if column order differs or some columns are missing. However, the header row in the first row is required.
failed to read detail: The following items could not be read because they were not in the correct format.
delete all message: Are you sure you want to delete all the Pokémon in the box?
deleted all pokemon: Deleted all the Pokémon in the box.
include pity proc: Include pity proc in skill calculation
pity proc help: Pokémon Sleep has a pity system where the main skill is guaranteed to trigger after a certain number of consecutive failures.
pity proc help2: When this flag is enabled, the number of main skill activations is calculated taking the pity system into account.
pity proc help3: If <name/>'s main skill fails to trigger <count/> times in a row after the last activation, it is guaranteed to trigger on attempt <count1/>.
pity proc ref: The pity proc information is referenced from <raenonx>RaenonX's article</raenonx>.
initialize all parameters message: Do you want to restore all parameters? Doing so will set the strength to the same value as in the [RP] tab.
recipe bonus help: Please specify the recipe bonus for frequently made meals. Recipe bonus typically depends on the number of ingredients, but some recipes may have higher bonus values. For details, see the <raenonx>Recipes / Dishes (RaenonX)</raenonx>.
recipe strength help: This value is used to convert ingredients into strength amounts, assuming 80% are used in the recipe and 20% are surplus.
charge energy not implemented: Currently, energy recovery via Charge Energy S is not included in the calculations.
berry juice assumption: The Berry Juice acquisition rate uses the RaenonX tentative value (20%).
help yield description: The average number of berries and ingredients collected over {{n}} helps.
helps for cap description: The average $t(help count) needed to reach the 999 $t(berry) and $t(ingredient) cap, with the effect of $t(skills.Helper Boost.name), after entering $t(sneaky snacking) mode due to a full $t(carry limit).
required helper boost description: The average number of $t(skills.Helper Boost.name) triggers needed to reach the 999 $t(berry) and $t(ingredient) cap, after entering $t(sneaky snacking) mode due to a full $t(carry limit).
all desc: $t(specialty): A Pokémon that has all the characteristics of $t(berry), $t(ingredient), and $t(skill).
berries desc: A Pokémon that excels at collecting berries, such as being able to pick up many berries in a single helping.
ingredients desc: A Pokémon that excels at collecting ingredients, such as being able to pick up many ingredients in a single helping.
skills desc: A Pokémon that excels at triggering skills, such as having a special main skill or a high trigger rate. It can stock one extra main skill and may trigger its main skill twice in a single helping.
name: Name
sufficient candy: You have enough Pokémon candies. No need to use handy candy or type candy.
helping bonus addition label: Include the strength gained by other members via [$t(subskill.Helping Bonus)] in total strength
strength detail1: You can check the strength, ingredients count, skill trigger count of your Pokémon.
strength detail2: Currently, there are constraints in the calculation logic as follows:
strength restriction2: Energy recovery via Charge Energy S is not included in the calculations.
skill not triggered: The main skill is not triggered in this setting.
rating detail1: We evaluate subskill and nature tendencies on a scale of 0 to 100 points. For example, in the case of 'berries', the subskill or nature that picks up the most berries are treated as 100 points. We calculate the score based on how much the amount of 'berries' your Pokémon picks up is compared to 100 points.
rating detail2: For Pokémon specialized in berries, it's desirable for their berry score to be high, and for Pokémon specialized in skills, it's desirable for their skill score to be high. Please understand that this is a simple scoring system because we do not consider the following information for the evaluation: Pokémon species, ingredients, skill levels, certain subskills (bonus or inventory), and certain natures (EXP gains or energy recovery).
